questions.text: Cnidarians are characterized by which of the following?I. AcoelomateII. Radial symmetryIII. Development of a head
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While cnidarians are acoelomate, they are also characterized by radial symmetry, which is missing in this option.
This option is incorrect. While cnidarians do exhibit radial symmetry, they are also acoelomate, which is not accounted for in this option.
This option is incorrect. Cnidarians do not develop a head; they have a decentralized nerve net.
This option is correct. Cnidarians are both acoelomate and exhibit radial symmetry. They do not have a centralized head structure.
This option is incorrect. While cnidarians are acoelomate and exhibit radial symmetry, they do not develop a head.

questions.explanation: Cnidarians are characterized by the following: I. Acoelomate: This is correct. Cnidarians are not coelomate. II. Radial symmetry: This is correct. Cnidarians exhibit radial symmetry, which means their body parts are arranged around a central axis, like the spokes of a wheel. This symmetry allows them to interact with their environment from all directions equally. III. Development of a head: This is incorrect. Cnidarians do not have a centralized head with a brain or complex sensory organs. They have a decentralized nerve net that allows them to coordinate simple responses to stimuli but not a true head. So, the correct answers are I and II. Radial symmetry and Acoelomate.

questions.text: Two snails in the same class must also be in the same:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: An order consists of multiple families. Snails in the same class can belong to different orders.
Species is the most specific classification. Two snails in the same class could be in different species.
A genus includes species with similar characteristics. Snails in the same class can belong to different genera.
A phylum is a higher taxonomic rank that includes multiple classes. Thus, snails in the same class must be in the same phylum.

questions.explanation: Taxon represents the taxonomic group of any rank. Each plant and animal belong to a hierarchy of taxa which are assigned taxonomic ranks. The seven essential ranks of taxa in descending order are the kingdom, division/phylum, class, order, family, genus and species. Two snails in the same class may belong to different order, species, genus and family but to the same phylum. This is because phylum includes classes sharing common features and two snails of the same class may be further assigned to same or different order, species, genus and family based on their similarities and dissimilarities. Thus, the correct answer is option E.

questions.text: Which of the following characteristics is mainly responsible for the diversification of insects on land?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The main characteristic responsible for the diversification of insects on land is the exoskeleton. The exoskeleton is a hard, protective covering that surrounds the insect's body. It is made of chitin, a tough, fibrous substance that helps to prevent the insect from drying out and from being damaged by predators. The exoskeleton also provides a place for muscles to attach, which allows insects to move quickly and easily.
Eyes allow insects to see their surroundings, which is important for finding food and avoiding predators.
Segmentation allows insects to move their body parts independently, which gives them more flexibility and maneuverability.
Bilateral symmetry means that the insect's body is divided into two mirror-image halves, which helps to protect the insect from injury.

questions.explanation: The exoskeleton, or outer skeleton, is a key characteristic of insects that has contributed significantly to their diversification on land. The exoskeleton provides structural support, protection, and resistance to desiccation, allowing insects to thrive in terrestrial environments. It also serves as a site for muscle attachment, enabling efficient movement and locomotion. The rigid exoskeleton of insects provides advantages such as protection against predators and the ability to exploit a wide range of ecological niches. Additionally, the exoskeleton undergoes molting, allowing for growth and development throughout an insect's life cycle. The unique properties and adaptability of the exoskeleton have played a major role in the successful colonization and diversification of insects on land.

questions.text: Cnidarians are characterized by which of the following?I. AcoelomateII. Radial symmetryIII. Development of a head
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While Cnidarians are indeed acoelomate, they also exhibit radial symmetry and develop a head-like region.
Radial symmetry is a key feature of Cnidarians, but they also lack a coelom and develop a mouth surrounded by tentacles.
Cnidarians do develop a head-like region with a mouth and tentacles, but they are also acoelomate and exhibit radial symmetry.
All three characteristics listed are features of Cnidarians.

questions.explanation: I. Acoelomate: This means they lack a coelom, a fluid-filled body cavity found in some animals between the body wall and the digestive system. Cnidarians have a simpler body structure and don't possess a coelom. II. Radial symmetry: Their body parts are arranged radially around a central axis. They can be divided into similar halves along multiple planes passing through the central axis. This is a common feature of cnidarians like jellyfish and sea anemones. III. Development of a head: While not as prominent as in bilaterally symmetrical animals, cnidarians do have a mouth opening surrounded by tentacles that function in feeding and defense. This region can be considered the "head" end, although it's not as complex as a true head with specialized sensory organs.Therefore, all three characteristics listed are features of Cnidarians.

questions.text: Which of the following is an endotherm?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Grasshoppers are ectothermic, relying on external environmental conditions to regulate their body temperature. They are not capable of internally regulating their temperature as endotherms do.
Hydras are simple aquatic organisms that are ectothermic, meaning they cannot regulate their internal temperature and depend on the environment to do so.
Earthworms are ectothermic organisms, depending on external sources of heat to regulate their body temperature.
Blue Jays are birds, which are endothermic organisms. They have the ability to maintain a stable internal body temperature through metabolic processes, independent of the external environment.

questions.explanation: Option A) Grasshopper is an ectothermic organism and not endothermic. The ectothermic organism is also called cold-blooded. They depend on the external environment to maintain their body temperature. They expose their head and body to the sun's rays and maintain body temperature.Option B) Hydra is an ectothermic organism or a cold-blooded organism. It also depends on the external environment to maintain body temperature. In Hydra, the body size is regulated by temperature via Wnt signalling.Option C) Earthworms are also ectothermic or cold-blooded organisms. They also depend on the external environment to maintain their body heat. They have temperature-sensing neurons that sense the heat, and that is how they reduce their body functions so that they can live longer even in increasing heat. Their reproduction also depends on temperature.Option D) Blue jay is an endothermic or warm-blooded organism. They maintain their body temperature independent of the external environment. Their metabolic process helps them to maintain temperature homeostasis. The blue jay is a bird, and all birds and mammals are warm-blooded organisms. They have special metabolic tissues that help regulate the internal temperature, or some can decrease the metabolic rate to preserve body heat.Hence, the correct answer is option (D)Note: Endotherms have a high metabolic rate compared to ectothermic organisms. Endotherms have brown adipose tissue or other metabolically active organs that maintain the internal body heat.

questions.text: What will you look for to identify the sex of the following?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Female Ascaris - sharply curved posterior end
options.explanation: Tail end is straight in female Ascaris, while tail end is curved towards ventral in male Ascaris.

options.text: Male frog - a copulatory pad on the first digit of the hind limb
options.explanation: The forelimbs in both frogs (male and female) bear small articular pads dorsally at the joints of digit, but the males possess a special nuptial, copulatory pad on ventral side of the first finger of each forelimb. Copulatory pads appear merely as rough patches, but during breeding season, they become thick and sticky. In copulation, the male strongly grips a female under her armpits by means of these pads.
Anal cerci is present in both male and female cockroach, while anal style is present only in the male cockroach.

options.text: Male shark - claspers borne on pelvic fins
options.explanation: Claspers are modified inner edges of pelvic fins in male sharks.Female sharks do not have claspers.

questions.explanation: Tail end is straight in female Ascaris, while tail end is curved towards ventral in male Ascaris. Anal cerci is present in both male and female cockroach, while anal style is present only in the male cockroach. The forelimbs in both frogs (male and female) bear small articular pads dorsally at the joints of digit, but the males possess a special nuptial, copulatory pad on ventral side of the first finger of each forelimb. Copulatory pads appear merely as rough patches, but during breeding season, they become thick and sticky. In copulation, the male strongly grips a female under her armpits by means of these pads. Claspers are modified inner edges of pelvic fins in male sharks.

questions.text: Paramecia are unicellular protists that have contractile vacuoles to remove excess intracellular water. In an experimental investigation, paramecia were placed in salt solutions of increasing osmolarity. The rate at which the contractile vacuole contracted to pump out excess water was determined and plotted against osmolarity of the solutions, as shown in the graph. Which of the following is the correct explanation for the data?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: At higher osmolarity, lower rates of contraction are required because more salt diffuses into the paramecia
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the contraction rate is more related to water movement than salt diffusion. Higher osmolarity outside means less water enters the paramecia, reducing the need for the vacuole to contract frequently.

options.text: The contraction rate increases as the osmolarity decreases because the amount of water entering the paramecia by osmosis increases
options.explanation: This is correct. As the surrounding solution becomes less concentrated (lower osmolarity), more water enters the paramecia, increasing the demand for the contractile vacuole to expel excess water.

options.text: The contractile vacuole is less efficient in solutions of high osmolarity because of the reduced amount of ATP produced from cellular respiration
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The efficiency of the contractile vacuole is not directly impacted by ATP production in this context. The contraction rate is more related to osmotic balance rather than ATP efficiency.

options.text: In an isosmotic salt solution, there is no diffusion of water into or out of the paramecia, so the contraction rate is zero
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While water movement is balanced in isosmotic conditions, some contraction may still occur for regular cellular processes and slight imbalances. It doesn't necessarily drop to zero.

questions.explanation: Based on the given graph and information provided, the correct explanation for the data is:B. The contraction rate increases as the osmolarity decreases because the amount of water entering the Paramecium by osmosis increases.The graph shows a diagonal line that starts at its highest point near the top of the y-axis and ends at its lowest point just above the x-axis. This pattern indicates an inverse relationship between the rate of contraction (y-axis) and the osmolarity of the solution (x-axis). As the osmolarity decreases, the rate of contraction increases.This observation suggests that as the salt concentration or osmolarity of the solution decreases, more water enters the Paramecium by osmosis. As a result, the contractile vacuole needs to work at a higher rate to remove the excess water from the cell, leading to an increased rate of contraction. In other words, the Paramecium responds to a hypotonic environment (lower osmolarity) by taking in more water, which necessitates a higher rate of contraction to maintain osmotic balance.Therefore, option B provides the correct explanation for the observed data.

questions.text: Body having a meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food filtering flagellated cells and indirect development are the characteristics of which Phylum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mollusca have a hard shell, a mantle, and a foot. Have true tissues and organs, not a meshwork of cells. No flagellated collar cells. Have direct or indirect development but not the described body plan.
Protozoa are single-celled organisms. No internal cavity or body made of cells — just one single cell.

Coelenterata have a body with two layers of cells and a central cavity. Have tissue level of organization and a gastrovascular cavity, not a system of canals and pores.Their body wall has cnidocytes (stinging cells), not flagellated collar cells.

Porifera, commonly known as sponges, exhibit the described characteristics. Sponges have a meshwork of cells called choanocytes that line their internal cavities. These choanocytes have flagella that create water currents to filter and capture food particles. Sponges also undergo indirect development, meaning they go through larval stages before reaching their adult form.

questions.explanation: The phylum that has a body having a meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food-filtering flagellated cells, and indirect development is Porifera. Porifera, also known as sponges, are the simplest multicellular animals. They are characterized by a meshwork of cells called the spongocoel, which is lined with flagellated cells called choanocytes. The choanocytes create a water current that brings food and oxygen into the sponge and carries away waste. Porifera also has an indirect development, meaning that the young form is different from the adult form.

questions.text: Which of the following are correctly matched with respect to their taxonomic classification?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Housefly, butterfly, Insecta tsetse fly, silverfish
options.explanation: Yes, houseflies, butterflies, tsetse flies, and silverfish all belong to the same phylum in the animal kingdom, which is Arthropoda. The phylum Arthropoda is characterized by having jointed legs and a segmented exoskeleton, and it includes a vast diversity of invertebrate animals, including insects (like houseflies, butterflies, and tsetse flies) and other arthropods (like silverfish).

options.text: Spiny anteater, sea urchin, sea cucumber
options.explanation: Spiny ant eater (Echidna) is a prototherian mammal whereas, sea urchins and sea cucumbers are echinoderms.

options.text: Flying fish, Cuttle fish, Pisces silver fish
options.explanation: Silver fish (Lepisma) is an insect, Cuttle fish (Sepia) is a mollusc and flying fish (Exocoetus) is a bony fish.
Centipede is class chilopoda, Millipede is class diplopoda and Scorpion and Spider are class arachnida of Phylum Arthropoda.

questions.explanation: Houseflies, butterflies, tsetse flies, and silverfish all belong to the same phylum in the animal kingdom, which is Arthropoda. Spiny ant eater (Echidna) is a prototherian mammal whereas, sea urchins and sea cucumbers are echinoderms. Silver fish (Lepisma) is an insect, Cuttle fish (Sepia) is a mollusc and flying fish (Exocoetus) is a bony fish. Centipede is class chilopoda, Millipede is class diplopoda and Scorpion and Spider are class arachnida of Phylum Arthropoda.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of items correctly belongs to the category of organs mentioned against it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nephridia of earthworm and Malpighian tubules of cockroach - excretory organs
options.explanation: Nephridia are excretory organs in earthworms, responsible for removing waste and excess water from the body. Malpighian tubules are excretory organs in cockroaches and some other insects, responsible for removing waste and maintaining the water balance in their bodies.

options.text: Wings of honey bee and wings of crow - homologous organs
options.explanation: Wings of honey bee and wings of crow are analogous organs as they are functionally similar, not structurally.

options.text: Thorn of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbita - analogous organs
options.explanation: Thorns and tendrils serve different functions and are not considered analogous organs in biological terms.

options.text: Nictitating membrane and blind spot in human eye - vestigial organs
options.explanation: Nictitating membrane is a vestigial structure (a reduced and non-functional organ that had a function in ancestors), and the blind spot is a natural feature in the human eye but not considered a vestigial organ.

questions.explanation: Nephridia are excretory organs in earthworms, responsible for removing waste and excess water from the body. Malpighian tubules are excretory organs in cockroaches and some other insects, responsible for removing waste and maintaining the water balance in their bodies.

questions.text: What is common between parrot, platypus and kangaroo?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parrots and duckbills (platypuses) both have toothless jaws. Parrots have beaks that lack teeth, and platypuses have a specialized jaw structure with horny plates for grinding food. Kangaroos, on the other hand, have complex molars for grinding plant material, and they are not considered toothless.
No, parrots, duckbills (platypuses), and kangaroos do not have functional postanal tails. While they are all vertebrates, they do not possess tails like those found in some other.
Kangaroois is viviparous.

Homoiothermy is the maintenance by an animal of its internal body temperature at a relatively constant value by using metabolic processes to counteract fluctuations in the temperature of the environment. Homoiothermy occurs in birds and mammals, which are described as endotherms. The heat produced by their tissue metabolism and the heat lost to the environment are balanced by various means to keep body temperature constant: 36-38ºC in mammals and 38-40ºC in birds. The hypothalamus in the brain monitors blood temperature and controls thermoregulation by both nervous and hormonal means. Thus, parrots (birds), platypuses and kangaroos (mammals) are homoiothermic animals.

questions.explanation: Homoiothermy is the maintenance by an animal of its internal body temperature at a relatively constant value by using metabolic processes to counteract fluctuations in the temperature of the environment. Homoiothermy occurs in birds and mammals, which are described as endotherms. The heat produced by their tissue metabolism and the heat lost to the environment are balanced by various means to keep body temperature constant: 36-38ºC in mammals and 38-40ºC in birds. The hypothalamus in the brain monitors blood temperature and controls thermoregulation by both nervous and hormonal means. Thus, parrots (birds), platypuses and kangaroos (mammals) are homoiothermic animals.

questions.text: Select the correct statement from the ones given below with respect to Periplaneta americana:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nervous system located is dorsally and consists of segmentally arranged ganglia joined by a pair of longitudinal connectives.
options.explanation: In cockroach, the nervous system is located ventrally, not dorsally.It does consist of segmentally arranged ganglia connected by paired longitudinal connectives, but since it is ventral, this statement is incorrect.

options.text: Males bear a pair of short thread like anal styles.
options.explanation: Male cockroaches possess a pair of short, thread-like anal styles — small appendages found below the anal cerci.Females lack these anal styles.Hence, this statement is correct.

options.text: There are 16 very long Malpighian tubules present at the junctions of the midgut and hindgut.
options.explanation: In Periplaneta americana, there are about 100–150 yellow-colored Malpighian tubules, not 16.They are found at the junction of midgut and hindgut, but the number given is wrong.

options.text: Grinding of food is carried out only by the mouth parts.
options.explanation: In cockroach, grinding occurs not only by mouthparts (mandibles) but also by the gizzard (proventriculus) in the foregut.The gizzard has chitinous teeth that further grind the food.So, saying “only by mouthparts” is wrong.

questions.explanation: Nutrition The posterior segment of cockroaches bears appendages called anal cerci. These are found in both male and female, but male cockroaches can be distinguished by female ones by the presence of an extra pair of accessory appendages called anal styles. These assist during copulation.

questions.text: Earthworms have no skeleton but during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and acts as a hydraulic skeleton.This is due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gut peristalsis is the wave-like movement of the alimentary canal that helps move food forward.It has nothing to do with the hydraulic skeleton or burrowing movement.

Setae are tiny bristle-like structures on each segment.They anchor the worm in the soil during movement but do not create turgidity or pressure.They help in traction, not in forming a hydrostatic skeleton.

The hydraulic skeleton is the system of support found in soft-bodied invertebrates which relies on the incompressibility of fluids contained within the body cavity. In earthworms the coelomic fluid is under pressure within the coelom and therefore provides support for internal organs. Due to the hydraulic skeleton, during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and aids in the relaxation of longitudinal muscles.
Blood in earthworms is confined to blood vessels (closed circulatory system).It functions in transport of gases and nutrients, not in support or rigidity.So, it does not form the hydraulic skeleton.

questions.explanation: The hydraulic skeleton is the system of support found in soft-bodied invertebrates which relies on the incompressibility of fluids contained within the body cavity. In earthworms the coelomic fluid is under pressure within the coelom and therefore provides support for internal organs. Due to the hydraulic skeleton, during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and aids in the relaxation of longitudinal muscles.

questions.text: If a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging its gut, the fluid that comes out is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid. It lies between body wall and alimentary canal. So if a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging the gut then only coelomic fluid will come out.
No, earthworms do not have haemolymph. They have a circulatory system with a fluid called coelomic fluid that serves functions similar to hemolymph in some other invertebrates.
Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid, not slimy mucus.
Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid, not excretory fluid.

questions.explanation: Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid. It lies between body wall and alimentary canal. So if a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging the gut then only coelomic fluid will come out.

questions.text: Which one of the following animals possesses nerve cells but no nerves?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hydra, belonging to the phylum Cnidaria, possesses a nerve net without centralized nerves. The nerve net allows for the coordination of simple responses to stimuli, such as touch or light changes.
Tapeworms are members of the phylum Platyhelminthes and have a rudimentary nervous system called a nerve ladder, composed of ganglia and nerve cords.
Earthworms, from the phylum Annelida, have a segmented nervous system with ganglia in each segment connected by a ventral nerve cord, allowing for more advanced movement and response coordination.
Frog tadpoles have a brain and spinal cord, which develops further during metamorphosis into an adult frog, resulting in a more complex central nervous system.

questions.explanation: Hydra, belonging to the phylum Cnidaria, possesses a nerve net without centralized nerves. The nerve net allows for the coordination of simple responses to stimuli, such as touch or light changes.

questions.text: Sharks and dogfishes differ from skates and rays because:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Their gill slits are ventrally placed
options.explanation: The gill slits of sharks and dogfishes are laterally placed, meaning they are located on the sides of their bodies, unlike in skates and rays where gill slits can be found ventrally.

options.text: Their head and trunk are widened considerably
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes do not have a considerably widened head and trunk. Instead, they have a streamlined body shape for efficient swimming, whereas skates and rays have a flattened body structure.

options.text: There is distinct demarcation between body and tail
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes have a streamlined body with no distinct separation between the body and tail, unlike some other animal groups. This is not a distinguishing feature between them and skates and rays.

options.text: Their pectoral fins distinctly marked off from cylindrical bodies
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes have distinct pectoral fins that are clearly separated from their cylindrical bodies, whereas skates and rays have pectoral fins that are fused to the body, giving them a wing-like appearance.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that sharks and dogfishes have their pectoral fins distinctly marked off from their cylindrical bodies. This is in contrast to skates and rays, whose pectoral fins are fused with the body, creating a wing-like structure. This distinction is crucial in differentiating these groups within the Chondrichthyes class. The other options are incorrect because they describe features that do not accurately differentiate sharks and dogfishes from skates and rays.

questions.text: 1. Fertilization_.2. Digestive system_.3. Excretion by_.Fill in the blanks for the organism given in the below figure:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While fertilization in flatworms (phylum Platyhelminthes) is indeed internal, they do not possess rennet cells. The term “blind end” refers to a saclike digestive system, but “rennet cells” are not involved in excretion in any invertebrate group. Hence, this option is not correct.

This option is also incorrect. Flatworms have internal fertilization, not external. Their digestive system is saclike, not tube-like (since it has only one opening serving as both mouth and anus). Additionally, they excrete through flame cells, not nephridia (which are found in annelids like earthworms).

This option is correct. Flatworms (Platyhelminthes) exhibit internal fertilization. Their digestive system is saclike, with a single opening for both ingestion and egestion. Excretion and osmoregulation are carried out by flame cells (also known as protonephridia). These features are characteristic of this phylum.

This option is partly correct because the digestive system of flatworms is indeed saclike, and excretion occurs through protonephridia, which contain flame cells. However, fertilization in flatworms is internal, not external. Thus, this option is incorrect overall

questions.explanation: Flatworms (Platyhelminthes) exhibit internal fertilization. Their digestive system is saclike, with a single opening for both ingestion and egestion. Excretion and osmoregulation are carried out by flame cells (also known as protonephridia). These features are characteristic of this phylum.

questions.text: During embryonic development the mouth is formed at some distance anterior to the blastopore and the blastopore forms the anus in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: When blastopore develops into anus then these animals are called deuterostomates. Echinoderms and chordates are deuterostomates.
Molluscs are protostomates , in them blastopore develops into mouth .
Nematods are pseudocoelomates therefore they are not grouped into protostomes and deuterostomes.
These are also pseudocoelomates.

questions.explanation: A. When blastopore develops into anus then these animals are called deuterostomates. Echinoderms and chordates are deuterostomates.B. Molluscs are protostomates , in them blastopore develops into mouth .C. Nematods are pseudocoelomates therefore they are not grouped into protostomes and deuterostomes.D. These are also pseudocoelomates.

questions.text: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Possess a solid single stranded central nervous system
options.explanation: Crocodiles and penguins possess a central nervous system, but it is not single-stranded; it includes a brain and spinal cord. In contrast, whales and dogfish also have a central nervous system but, like other vertebrates, they have a brain and spinal cord as well.

options.text: Lay eggs and guard them till they hatch
options.explanation: Crocodiles and penguins are similar in that they lay eggs and guard them, but whales and dogfish give birth to live young instead of laying eggs. So, there is a distinction between these groups in their reproductive methods.
Dogfish, also known as the spiny dogfish, is a type of cartilaginous fish and does not possess a bony skeleton like crocodiles, penguins, and whales.

options.text: Have gill slits at some stage
options.explanation: Animals belonging to Phylum Chordata are fundamentally characterised by the presence of a notochord, a dorsal hollow nerve cord and paired pharyngeal gill slits. Crocodile, penguin, whale and dogfish are all chordates. All of them have gill slits or have had them during embryonic development. Thus, paired gill slits are present in these animals at some stage of life.

questions.explanation: Animals belonging to Phylum Chordata are fundamentally characterised by the presence of a notochord, a dorsal hollow nerve cord and paired pharyngeal gill slits. Crocodile, penguin, whale and dogfish are all chordates. All of them have gill slits or have had them during embryonic development. Thus, paired gill slits are present in these animals at some stage of life.

questions.text: Name of common gut roundworm parasite of humans and pigs.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides is a common intestinal roundworm parasite found in humans (and a closely related species, Ascaris suum, infects pigs). These worms live in the lumen of the small intestine, usually in the jejunum or ileum. Infection occurs through ingestion of eggs from contaminated food or water. The larvae migrate through the lungs before returning to the intestine to mature. This is the correct answer.

Pheretima posthuma is a species of earthworm, commonly found in moist soil. It plays an important role in aerating the soil and decomposing organic matter. It is not parasitic and does not infect humans or pigs. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Lumbricus terrestris is another species of earthworm, also non-parasitic. It lives freely in soil and helps improve soil fertility by burrowing and digesting decaying material. It does not live in the human or pig intestine, so this is incorrect.

Hirudo medicinalis is the medicinal leech, a segmented annelid that feeds on the blood of vertebrates. It is used in medicine for therapeutic purposes but is not a gut roundworm parasite. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Ascaris worms inhabit the lumen of the small intestine, usually in the jejunum or ileus. They have a life span of 10 months to 2 years and then are passed in the stool.

questions.text: Birds do not have teeth so the function of teeth is performed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Birds use their beaks for a variety of tasks like grasping and eating food. However, the beak is not specialized for grinding food like teeth.
The pharynx is involved in the passage of food and air but does not perform any grinding or crushing functions.
The stomach is essential for digestion using acids and enzymes, but it does not mechanically grind food like teeth do.
The gizzard is a specialized, muscular part of the bird's digestive system that grinds and crushes food, effectively substituting the function of teeth.

questions.explanation: The gizzard is a specialized, muscular part of the bird's digestive system that grinds and crushes food, effectively substituting the function of teeth.

questions.text: Which one of the following is one of the paths followed by air or oxygen during respiration in the adult male Periplaneta Americana as it enters the animal body?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Spiracle in metathorax, trachea, tracheloes, oxygen diffuses into cells
options.explanation: Cockroaches utilize a tracheal system for respiration. Air enters through spiracles located on the thorax and abdomen, with the metathoracic spiracle being a key point of entry. The air travels through tracheae and tracheoles, facilitating direct oxygen diffusion into cells.

options.text: Mouth, bronchial tube, trachea, oxygen enters cells
options.explanation: Unlike mammals, cockroaches do not use a mouth or bronchial tubes for respiration. Their system consists of spiracles, tracheae, and tracheoles, which bypasses the need for such structures.

options.text: Spiracles in prothorax, tracheoles, trachea, oxygen diffuses into cells
options.explanation: While spiracles are essential for respiration, the primary spiracles for air entry in Periplaneta americana are located in the metathorax, not the prothorax.
The hypopharynx and mouth are involved in feeding, not respiration, in cockroaches. Their respiration relies on the tracheal system that completely bypasses these structures.

questions.explanation: The respiratory system of Periplaneta americana, or the American cockroach, is distinct from that of mammals. It consists of a network of tracheae and tracheoles through which air is distributed directly to the cells. Air enters the body through spiracles, small openings on the thorax and abdomen. Specifically, the metathoracic spiracle is one of the primary entry points in the thorax. This system compensates for the cockroach's less efficient circulatory system by directly delivering oxygen to tissues. Other options incorrectly describe a mammalian respiratory path or incorrectly locate the spiracles.

questions.text: Both male and female pigeons secrete milk through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Male and female pigeons both produce a substance often referred to as "pigeon milk," which is a secretion from the lining of their crops (not salivary glands).
Pigeons produce "pigeon milk" through modified cells in their crop, not the adrenal glands.

Pigeons are noted for their unique ability to produce “pigeon’s milk”, a soft, cheesy and nourishing secretion, especially during the breeding season. It is formed by the degeneration of the epithelial cells lining the crop. It is regurgitated into the mouth of the young birds until they are old enough to manage a graindiet like their parents. The pigeon’s milk includes water, fat, protein (casein) and lactose. The milk is produced by both sexes and contains 35 percent of fat.

Both male and female pigeons secrete "pigeon milk" through a specialized tissue lining in their crop, not through the gizzard. The gizzard is a muscular part of a bird's stomach that aids in the mechanical breakdown of food.

questions.explanation: Pigeons are noted for their unique ability to produce “pigeon’s milk”, a soft, cheesy and nourishing secretion, especially during the breeding season. It is formed by the degeneration of the epithelial cells lining the crop. It is regurgitated into the mouth of the young birds until they are old enough to manage a graindiet like their parents. The pigeon’s milk includes water, fat, protein (casein) and lactose. The milk is produced by both sexes and contains 35 percent of fat.

questions.text: Eutherians are characterised by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: All mammals have hairy skin as a characteristic feature. Hair is a defining characteristic of the class Mammalia, which includes a wide variety of species ranging from rodents and bats to whales and humans.

Eutheria is a taxon containing the placental mammals, such as humans. Nevertheless, all Eutherians are placental mammals. This means that a Eutherian foetus is nourished during gestation by a placenta. Eutherians are also viviparous, meaning that the offspring are carried in the mother's womb until fully developed.
Ovoviviparity is a reproductive mode found in some mammals, including certain species within the class Mammalia.
This is incorrect. Eutherians don't have glandular skin.

questions.explanation: Eutheria is a taxon containing the placental mammals, such as humans. Nevertheless, all Eutherians are placental mammals. This means that a Eutherian foetus is nourished during gestation by a placenta. Eutherians are also viviparous, meaning that the offspring are carried in the mother's womb until fully developed.

questions.text: The table shows some characteristics of four different vertebrates. Which vertebrate is a reptile?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chordate's skin has embedded dermal scales which may be ganoid, cycloid, or ctenoid scales. Fins both, median (single) or paired and have rays of cartilage or bone.
Reptiles have dry scaly skin which is adapted to land life. Most reptiles have better-developed limbs well adapted for efficient locomotion.
Amphibians, like frogs, toads, and salamanders, possess legs for locomotion on land or in water but typically do not have scales or hair.
The vertebrates possessing legs and hair but lacking fins and scales are mammals.

questions.explanation: Characteristics of Reptiles:✓ Reptiles are four-legged vertebrate animals.✓ Most reptiles lay eggs.The skin of reptiles is covered with scales. ✓ Reptiles have cold-blooded metabolisms. ✓ Reptiles breathe with the aid of lungs.

questions.text: Match the following:Column IA. AscarisB. OctopusC. AncylostomaD. PheretimaColumn II1. Intestinal round worm2. Mollusca3. Hook worm4. Earth worm
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option incorrectly matches Ascaris with Mollusca, Octopus with Earthworm, Ancylostoma with Hookworm, and Pheretima with Intestinal roundworm, which are incorrect classifications.
This option incorrectly matches Ascaris with Earthworm, Octopus with Hookworm, Ancylostoma with Intestinal roundworm, and Pheretima with Mollusca, which are incorrect classifications.

Correctly matches Ascaris with Intestinal roundworm, Octopus with Mollusca, Ancylostoma with Hookworm, and Pheretima with Earthworm. These are accurate biological classifications based on the characteristics of each organism.
This option incorrectly matches Ascaris with Mollusca, Octopus with Intestinal roundworm, Ancylostoma with Earthworm, and Pheretima with Hookworm, which are incorrect classifications.

questions.explanation: In the correct option, Ascaris is matched with 'Intestinal roundworm' (A-1) because it is a parasitic roundworm found in the intestines. Octopus is matched with 'Mollusca' (B-2) as it belongs to the phylum Mollusca. Ancylostoma is matched with 'Hookworm' (C-3) as it is a type of hookworm that can infect the intestines. Pheretima is matched with 'Earthworm' (D-4) because it is a genus of earthworms. The other options incorrectly assign these organisms to the wrong categories.So C is the correct option.

questions.text: What is the orgin of the acoelomate gut?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The ectoderm primarily forms structures such as the skin and nervous system, not the gut.
The mesoderm gives rise to muscles, bones, and the circulatory system, not the gut.
The endoderm is responsible for forming the gut and associated structures, making this the correct answer.
This option is incorrect because the gut is indeed derived from one of the embryonic layers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the acoelomate gut is derived from the endodermal layer. During embryonic development, the endoderm forms the lining of the gut and other internal organs. The ectoderm forms the outer structures like skin and the nervous system, while the mesoderm forms connective tissues such as muscle and bone. Thus, the endodermal origin of the gut is the accurate response to this question.

questions.text: Which of the following are the first groups of invertebrates which have developed a closed circulatory system?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nematodes are roundworms. They have an open circulatory system, which means that their blood flows freely through the body cavity.
The first groups of invertebrates which have developed a closed circulatory system are Annelids.
Arthropods have an open circulatory system as well. Their blood flows through a series of vessels and sinuses, but it does not circulate through a heart.

Molluscs have a closed circulatory system, but it is not as complex as the closed circulatory system of annelids. The blood of mollusks flows through a heart and a series of vessels, but it does not circulate through a closed system of capillaries.

questions.explanation: The first groups of invertebrates which have developed a closed circulatory system are Annelids.

questions.text: Which of the following is not associated to exoskeleton of arthropods?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While the exoskeleton is non-living, this characteristic does not indicate the layer responsible for secreting it.
The exoskeleton is not secreted by the endodermis. It is actually secreted by the epidermis, the outermost layer of cells in arthropods.
The exoskeleton is indeed acellular as it is a non-living structure primarily composed of chitin.
The exoskeleton is composed of chitin, a polysaccharide, making this a correct association.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Secreted by endodermis' because the exoskeleton of arthropods is secreted by the epidermis, not the endodermis. The epidermis is the outermost layer of cells that produce the chitinous exoskeleton. As arthropods grow, they undergo ecdysis, shedding their old exoskeleton and forming a new one.Option A, 'Non-living,' is incorrect because while the exoskeleton is non-living, this does not relate to the biological layer that secretes it. Option C, 'Acellular,' is incorrect as the exoskeleton is indeed an acellular, non-living structure. Option D, 'Polysaccharide in nature,' is incorrect because the exoskeleton is made of chitin, a polysaccharide, thus correctly associated.

questions.text: Haemoglobin is found in plasma of :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Earthworms have haemoglobin dissolved in their blood plasma, not within cells.
Sparrows, like most vertebrates, have haemoglobin within their red blood cells, not in the plasma.
In humans, haemoglobin is found within red blood cells that float in the blood plasma.
Lizards have haemoglobin contained within their red blood cells, similar to other vertebrates.

questions.explanation: Haemoglobin is a protein found in the red blood cells of many animals, including humans. It is responsible for transporting oxygen from the lungs to the rest of the body and returning carbon dioxide from the body to the lungs. In humans, haemoglobin is contained within the red blood cells, which are suspended in the blood plasma. While earthworms do have haemoglobin, it is dissolved in their blood plasma, unlike in humans where it is contained within cells. Birds and reptiles, such as sparrows and lizards, also have red blood cells that contain haemoglobin, rather than having it directly in the plasma.

questions.text: Which one of the following organisms is scientifically correctly named, correctly printed according to the International Rules of Nomenclature, and correctly described?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Musca domestica is the scientific name of the common housefly, so it should be written in italics.

options.text: Plasmodium falciparum—a protozoan pathogen causing the most serious type of malaria.
options.explanation: Plasmodium falciparum is a protozoan parasite, one of the species of Plasmodium that causes malaria in humans. Being digenetic, its life cycle is complete in two hosts: man and mosquito. Its sexual cycle is completed in the female Anopheles mosquito and infective individuals called sporozoites are formed. These are transmitted to humans with the bite of an infected female Anopheles. The asexual cycle is passed in man in two phases. Malaria caused by P. falciparum (also known as aestivoautumnal, malignant tertian, or pernicious malaria) is the most dangerous form of malaria, with the highest rate of complications and mortality. In this case, the fever cycle is of 48 hours and is often fatal to the patient as it affects the brain.

options.text: Felis tigris—the Indian tiger, well protected in Gir forests
options.explanation: The scientific name of the Indian tiger is Felis tigris, so the the 1st letter should be capitalized.

options.text: E. coli—full name Entamoeba coli, a commonly occurring bacterium in the human intestine
options.explanation: The full name of E. coli is Escherichia coli.

questions.explanation: Plasmodium falciparum is a protozoan parasite and the most dangerous cause of malaria in humans. Its digenetic life cycle occurs in two hosts: humans, where the asexual cycle takes place, and the female Anopheles mosquito, where the sexual cycle occurs and infective sporozoites are formed. Transmission to humans happens through the bite of an infected mosquito. In humans, the parasite multiplies asexually, producing fever every 48 hours, and the disease, known as malignant tertian malaria, can be fatal as it often affects the brain. The scientific names of some common organisms are house lizard – Hemidactylus, housefly – Musca domestica, Indian tiger – Panthera tigris, and E. coli – Escherichia coli.

questions.text: The main blood vessel of arthropods usually:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This statement is accurate. The main blood vessel in arthropods, the dorsal vessel, typically lies on the dorsal (upper) side of the arthropod's body. Hence, this option is correct.
This is not the usual location of the main blood vessel in arthropods. It is typically on the dorsal side.
The main blood vessel does not typically run along the lateral (side) of the arthropod's body.

Arthropods do have a circulatory system with a main blood vessel (dorsal vessel) that pumps hemolymph, which is functionally similar to blood in vertebrates, throughout their bodies. Therefore, the main blood vessel is present in arthropods.

questions.explanation: This statement is correct. In arthropods, the main blood vessel is the dorsal vessel, which runs along the dorsal (upper) side of the body. It functions as the heart and helps circulate hemolymph throughout the body. Therefore, A option is accurate.

questions.text: Which important characteristic do hemichordates share with chordates?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nervous system of hemichordates is intraepidermal and includes a ventral nerve cord, which is not a shared characteristic with chordates.
Both hemichordates and chordates have pharyngeal gill slits, making this a shared characteristic. In hemichordates, they are dorsal, while in chordates, they are lateral.
This option is incorrect as both hemichordates and chordates are characterized by having pharyngeal gill slits.
Hemichordates lack a true notochord, unlike chordates, so this is not a shared feature.

questions.explanation: An important characteristic that hemichordates and chordates share is the presence of pharyngeal gill slits. Gill slits are dorsal in position in hemichordates whereas they are lateral in chordates. A true notochord does not occur in hemichordates. The nervous system is distinct from the vertebrate type being intraepidermal in position and having a ventral nerve cord.

questions.text: Which one of the following characteristics is not shared by birds and mammals?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viviparity refers to the ability of an organism to give birth to live young. While some mammals are viviparous (e.g., humans, dogs, and cats), birds are not. Birds reproduce by laying eggs, which undergo external development. Therefore, viviparity is a characteristic shared by mammals but not birds.On the other hand, options B, C, and D are characteristics shared by both birds and mammals. Both birds and mammals are warm-blooded, meaning they can maintain a relatively constant body temperature. They also have an ossified endoskeleton made of bones, which provides support and protection. Additionally, both birds and mammals have lungs for breathing, enabling them to extract oxygen from the air.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals are endothermic (warm-blooded), meaning they regulate their body temperature internally.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals have an ossified (bony) endoskeleton, although birds have lightweight, hollow bones adapted for flight.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals breathe using lungs, though birds have a more advanced air sac system for efficient respiration.

questions.explanation: Viviparity refers to the ability of an organism to give birth to live young. While some mammals are viviparous (e.g., humans, dogs, and cats), birds are not. Birds reproduce by laying eggs, which undergo external development. Therefore, viviparity is a characteristic shared by mammals but not birds.On the other hand, options B, C, and D are characteristics shared by both birds and mammals. Both birds and mammals are warm-blooded, meaning they can maintain a relatively constant body temperature. They also have an ossified endoskeleton made of bones, which provides support and protection. Additionally, both birds and mammals have lungs for breathing, enabling them to extract oxygen from the air.

questions.text: Choose the correct statement.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nociceptors are specialized receptors that primarily respond to pain stimuli, not pressure. This statement is incorrect.
Meissner's corpuscles are actually mechanoreceptors that detect light touch rather than temperature changes. This statement is incorrect.

options.text: Photoreceptors are depolarized in darkness and hyperpolarized in light
options.explanation: This statement accurately describes the function of photoreceptors. In the absence of light, photoreceptors are depolarized, while exposure to light causes them to hyperpolarize.

options.text: Receptors do not produce graded potentials
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Most sensory receptors do produce graded potentials, which are changes in the membrane potential in response to stimuli.

questions.explanation: In the dark, photoreceptors remain depolarized; light causes hyperpolarization, reducing neurotransmitter release.

questions.text: Which of the following characteristic features always holds true for the corresponding group of animals?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Possess a mouth with an upper and a lower jaw - Chordata
options.explanation: Phylum Chordata includes both jawless vertebrates (Agnatha) and jawed vertebrates (Gnathostomata).

options.text: 3–chambered heart with one incompletely divided ventricle - Reptilia
options.explanation: Crocodile is a reptile having four chambered heart.

Yes, chondrichthyes, a class of fish that includes sharks, rays, and chimaeras, have a cartilaginous endoskeleton. This means their internal skeletal structure is made of cartilage rather than bone, providing flexibility and reduced weight for life in the water.
Duck billed platypus and spiny anteater are oviparous mammals.

questions.explanation: Chondrichthyes, a class of fish that includes sharks, rays, and chimaeras, have a cartilaginous endoskeleton. This means their internal skeletal structure is made of cartilage rather than bone, providing flexibility and reduced weight for life in the water. Phylum Chordata includes both jawless vertebrates (Agnatha) and jawed vertebrates (Gnathostomata). Crocodile in Reptilia have a four chambered heart with two auricles and two ventricles. Duck billed platypus and spiny anteater are oviparous mammals.

questions.text: Which of the following features is not present in the Phylum Arthropoda?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parapodia are flattened, fleshy, vertical flap like outgrowths of body wall found in annelids on lateral sides of trunk segments. These are hollow structures enclosing coelom which is continuous with that of trunk segments. These serve the dual purpose of locomotion and respiration.
Arthropods possess jointed appendages, which are segmented and hinged structures that allow for versatile and flexible movement, such as walking, grasping, and feeding.
Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton, a tough, protective outer covering made of chitin, which provides support, protection, and serves as a surface for muscle attachment.
Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton, a tough, protective outer covering made of chitin, which provides support, protection, and serves as a surface for muscle attachment.

questions.explanation: Parapodia are flattened, fleshy, vertical flap like outgrowths of body wall found in annelids on lateral sides of trunk segments. These are hollow structures enclosing coelom which is continuous with that of trunk segments. These serve the dual purpose of locomotion and respiration.

questions.text: Which of the following animals is not viviparous?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Duckbilled platypus is an egg laying mammal. It is found in the rivers in eastern Australia and Tasmania. It is a beaver like monotreme about 5060 cm long and well adapted to live in water. Usually, two eggs are laid at a time. The female curls around them for incubation and remains inactive for about two weeks. Newly hatched young ones are very immature, naked, blind and each is 2.5 cm long.
Whales are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.
Bats are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.
Elephants are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.

questions.explanation: Platypus is a monotreme, a special mammal that lays eggs instead of giving birth to live young. Whales, bats (flying fox), and elephants are all viviparous mammals that give birth to live young.

questions.text: Which of the following characteristics is mainly responsible for the diversification of insects on land?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The main characteristic responsible for the diversification of insects on land is the exoskeleton. The exoskeleton is a hard, protective covering that surrounds the insect's body. It is made of chitin, a tough, fibrous substance that helps to prevent the insect from drying out and from being damaged by predators. The exoskeleton also provides a place for muscles to attach, which allows insects to move quickly and easily.
Eyes allow insects to see their surroundings, which is important for finding food and avoiding predators.
Segmentation allows insects to move their body parts independently, which gives them more flexibility and maneuverability.
Bilateral symmetry means that the insect's body is divided into two mirror-image halves, which helps to protect the insect from injury.

questions.explanation: The exoskeleton, or outer skeleton, is a key characteristic of insects that has contributed significantly to their diversification on land. The exoskeleton provides structural support, protection, and resistance to desiccation, allowing insects to thrive in terrestrial environments. It also serves as a site for muscle attachment, enabling efficient movement and locomotion. The rigid exoskeleton of insects provides advantages such as protection against predators and the ability to exploit a wide range of ecological niches. Additionally, the exoskeleton undergoes molting, allowing for growth and development throughout an insect's life cycle. The unique properties and adaptability of the exoskeleton have played a major role in the successful colonization and diversification of insects on land.

questions.text: One of the representatives of Phylum Arthropoda is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Puffer fish and flying fish (Exocoetus) are examples of superclass Pisces.
Puffer fish and flying fish (Exocoetus) are examples of superclass Pisces.
Cuttle fish (Sepia) belongs to Mollusca.
Phylum Arthropoda is the largest phylum of Animalia which includes insects. Examples include Apis, silkworm, Laccifer, silver fish (Lepisma), locust, etc.

questions.explanation: Phylum Arthropoda is the largest phylum of Animalia which includes insects. Examples include Apis, silkworm, Laccifer, silver fish (Lepisma), locust, etc. Puffer fish and flying fish (Exocoetus) are examples of superclass Pisces, while cuttle fish (Sepia) belongs to Mollusca.

questions.text: The characteristics of Class Reptilia are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Body covered with moist skin which is devoid of scales, the ear is represented by a tympanum, alimentary canal, urinary and reproductive tracts open into a common cloaca
options.explanation: These characteristics describe Class Amphibia, which are adapted to both aquatic and terrestrial environments but have moist skin without scales.

options.text: Fresh water animals with bony endoskeleton, airbladder to regulate buoyancy
options.explanation: These are the characteristics of Class Osteichthyes, or bony fish, which primarily live in aquatic environments and have a bony endoskeleton and an air bladder.

options.text: Marine animals with cartilaginous endoskeleton, body covered with placoid scales
options.explanation: These characteristics describe Class Chondrichthyes, which includes cartilaginous fish such as sharks and rays, known for their marine habitat and placoid scales.

options.text: Body covered with dry and cornified skin, scales over the body are epidermal, they do not have external ears
options.explanation: These are the defining characteristics of Class Reptilia, which are adapted for terrestrial life with dry, cornified skin and lack external ears.

questions.explanation: These are the defining characteristics of Class Reptilia, which are adapted for terrestrial life with dry, cornified skin and lack external ears.

questions.text: Which one of the following groups of animals reproduces only by sexual means?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cnidaria, such as jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones, can reproduce both sexually and asexually. For example, the medusa stage typically engages in sexual reproduction, while the polyp stage often reproduces asexually through budding.

Porifera, or sponges, are capable of both sexual and asexual reproduction. Asexual reproduction occurs through budding or fragmentation, while sexual reproduction occurs with the production of gametes as they are hermaphroditic.

Protozoa exhibit both sexual and asexual reproduction. Methods of asexual reproduction include binary fission and budding, while sexual reproduction can involve processes like syngamy and conjugation, depending on the species.

Ctenophores, also known as comb jellies, reproduce exclusively through sexual means. They are typically hermaphroditic, with external fertilization being the norm. Unlike the other groups mentioned, ctenophores do not reproduce asexually.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Ctenophora. Ctenophores, or comb jellies, reproduce solely through sexual reproduction and lack any form of asexual reproduction. They are monoecious, meaning individual organisms have both male and female reproductive organs, allowing for sexual reproduction to take place externally. In contrast, Cnidaria can reproduce both sexually (in the medusa stage) and asexually (in the polyp stage). Porifera, or sponges, can also reproduce both ways, with asexual reproduction occurring through mechanisms like budding. Similarly, Protozoa can reproduce both sexually and asexually, using methods such as binary fission and conjugation depending on the species and environmental conditions.

questions.text: Which one of the following animals is correctly matched with its one characteristic and the taxon?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Millipedes belong to the class Diplopoda within the phylum Arthropoda, not Arachnida. Although millipedes possess a ventral nerve cord, Arachnida is a different class that includes creatures like spiders and scorpions.

This option is incorrect. Sea anemones are part of the phylum Cnidaria, which is characterized by being diploblastic. They have two germ layers rather than three, distinguishing them from triploblastic animals.

options.text: Silverfish - Pectoral and Pelvic Fins - Chordata
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Silverfish are insects under the class Insecta within the phylum Arthropoda. They lack pectoral and pelvic fins, which are typical of some aquatic chordates like fish.
This option is correct. The duckbilled platypus is a distinctive mammal under the order Monotremata in the class Mammalia. It is oviparous, meaning it lays eggs, a rare trait among mammals.

questions.explanation: The duckbilled platypus is a distinctive mammal under the order Monotremata in the class Mammalia. It is oviparous, meaning it lays eggs, a rare trait among mammals.

questions.text: Which of the following are pseudocoelomates?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Flatworms are acoelomates, meaning no body cavity.They have solid bodies with only tissues between gut and body wall.So, they are not pseudocoelomates.
Echinoderms (starfish, sea urchins) are triploblastic coelomates.They have a true coelom, completely lined by mesoderm.Not pseudocoelomates.
Molluscs (snails, clams, squids) are triploblastic coelomates.Their body cavity (coelom) is well-developed and mesoderm-lined.So, they are not pseudocoelomates.
Aschelminthes (roundworms, nematodes) are pseudocoelomates.They have a body cavity called pseudocoel, partially lined by mesoderm.This cavity acts as a hydrostatic skeleton aiding in movement.

questions.explanation: Aschelminthes (roundworms, nematodes) are pseudocoelomates.They have a body cavity called pseudocoel, partially lined by mesoderm.This cavity acts as a hydrostatic skeleton aiding in movement.

questions.text: A body cavity is absent in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomates lack a body cavity, or coelom, between the body wall and the digestive system. This group includes the phylum Platyhelminthes, known as flatworms.
Pseudocoelomates possess a pseudocoelom, a body cavity that is not fully lined with mesoderm. Examples include the phylum Nematoda (roundworms).
This option is incorrect because pseudocoelomates do possess a type of body cavity, although it is not a true coelom.
Coelomates possess a true coelom, a body cavity fully lined with mesoderm. This group includes most animals, such as annelids, mollusks, and vertebrates.

questions.explanation: Acoelomates lack a body cavity, or coelom, between the body wall and the digestive system. This group includes the phylum Platyhelminthes, known as flatworms.

questions.text: Acoelomates do not lack:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomates lack a coelom.That’s the main defining feature of acoelomates.
Acoelomates are triploblastic animals in which all the three germinal layers ectoderm. mesoderm and endoderm are present.
Acoelomates do not have a specialized transport system.Transport occurs directly via diffusion because of their thin, flat bodies.
Acoelomates do not have a coelom, hence no coelomic fluid.

questions.explanation: Acoelomates are triploblastic animals in which all the three germinal layers ectoderm. mesoderm and endoderm are present.

questions.text: Everything is true about coelom except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Found between body wall and gut
options.explanation: The coelom lies between the outer body wall (ectoderm) and the gut wall (endoderm).This is the main definition of a coelom.
The coelomic cavity contains coelomic fluid, which helps in:Transport of nutrients and gases.Providing a hydrostatic skeleton in some animals.

The key feature of a true coelom is that it is completely lined by mesodermal epithelium (called peritoneum).That’s why animals with a true coelom are called coelomates (like annelids, mollusks, arthropods, echinoderms, chordates).

options.text: Bound internally by cuticle of intestine
options.explanation: Coelom is internally lined by visceral layer of mesoderm. In pseudocoelomates body cavity is internally bound by cuticle of intestine.

questions.explanation: Coelom is internally lined by visceral layer of mesoderm. In pseudocoelomates body cavity is internally bound by cuticle of intestine.

questions.text: A fluid filled cavity which is mesodermal in origin but absent in nematodes is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The coelom is a true body cavity that is completely lined by mesodermal tissue. It is absent in nematodes, which instead possess a pseudocoelom.
The gastrocoel is the primitive gut cavity formed during embryonic development, not a body cavity related to mesodermal tissue.
A pseudocoelom is a body cavity that is not entirely lined by mesodermal tissue. Nematodes possess a pseudocoelom, which is why this option is incorrect.
The spongocoel is the central cavity found in sponges, unrelated to mesodermal origins or nematodes.

questions.explanation: The coelom is a true body cavity that is entirely lined by mesodermal tissue, providing space for the development and suspension of organs. Nematodes, however, are classified as pseudocoelomates, meaning their body cavity is not completely lined by mesoderm. This distinguishes them from coelomates, which have a fully mesoderm-lined cavity. Other options like the gastrocoel and spongocoel refer to different types of cavities unrelated to the mesodermal origin, while the pseudocoelom, although a body cavity, lacks a complete mesodermal lining and is present in nematodes.
